name: Certain Lending
description_short: Certain Lending specializes in fix-and-flip, construction, and rental property loans for real estate investors, offering flexible terms and fast closing in 5 days to 3 weeks.
description_long:
Certain Lending is a specialized mortgage lender focused exclusively on real estate investment properties rather than owner-occupied homes. The company operates in the non-QM (non-qualified mortgage) space, serving experienced and emerging real estate investors who need short-term construction financing, fix-and-flip loans, permanent rental property financing, and portfolio loans. Founded to serve the investment property market, Certain Lending emphasizes speed, flexibility, and investor-friendly underwriting.

Certain Lending offers five primary loan products: Construction loans (12-24 months, up to $1.5M, 85% LTC), Fix-and-Flip loans (6-18 months, $100K-$1.5M, up to 92.5% LTC with no interest on undrawn funds), Rental Property loans (30-year amortizing, $75K-$1.5M, up to 80% LTV), Short-Term Rental loans for Airbnb/VRBO properties (30-year, up to 75% LTV), and Rental Portfolio loans allowing financing of up to 10 properties under a single loan. Loan amounts range from $75,000 to $1.5 million depending on product type, with FICO minimums between 660-700.

Certain Lending distinguishes itself through rapid closings (as fast as 5 days for fix-and-flip), streamlined underwriting that requires no employment or income verification, flexible draw schedules with funding in 1-3 business days post-inspection, and allowances for foreign nationals at reduced LTV. The company accepts virtual inspections, allows 100% financing of renovation and construction budgets, and bases pricing on borrower experience rather than traditional employment criteria. They explicitly service investment-property focused borrowers that traditional mortgage lenders often decline.

However, investors should note that Certain Lending exclusively finances investment properties—owner-occupied homes are not eligible. Rural properties are excluded from most products. All loans require entity structures with personal guarantees, and borrowers must have clean credit histories (no recent foreclosures or bankruptcies). While the lack of employment verification is marketed as a feature, it reflects the non-QM nature of these loans, which typically carry higher rates and fees than traditional mortgages. Portfolio loans require experience and a minimum property count.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Extremely fast closings: fix-and-flip loans close in as little as 5 days; construction/rental loans in 2-3 weeks
No employment or income verification required, enabling borrowers with non-traditional income to qualify
Flexible draw funding: 1-3 business days from inspection to draw, with reimbursement for materials, labor, and demolition
100% financing of renovation/construction budgets for qualified borrowers, minimizing out-of-pocket costs
No interest accrued on undrawn funds for fix-and-flip loans, reducing carrying costs during slower work phases
Portfolio loans allow financing up to 10 rental properties under a single loan, reducing closing costs vs. individual loans
Foreign nationals welcome at reduced LTV, expanding access to international investors
Virtual inspections available, reducing logistical barriers for out-of-state investors
cons: Investment properties only—owner-occupied homes are not eligible, limiting the borrower pool significantly
Rural properties explicitly excluded from most loan products, restricting geographic flexibility
Requires entity-based ownership with personal guarantees, adding complexity and legal costs for small investors
Non-QM loans typically carry higher interest rates and fees than traditional mortgages due to underwriting risk
Minimum FICO scores (660-700) are modest, and 'no recent foreclosures or bankruptcies' language is vague regarding timeframes
best_for: Real estate investors focusing on fix-and-flip projects who need capital fast and have strong exit timelines
Construction-focused investors building new residential or small multifamily investment properties
Rental property investors with stabilized, cash-flowing properties seeking long-term permanent financing
Short-term rental (Airbnb/VRBO) investors needing underwriting based on actual rental history rather than traditional employment
diagnosis: Certain Lending is best for experienced and emerging real estate investors seeking rapid, non-traditional underwriting for investment properties. The critical caveat is that this is exclusively an investment property lender—owner-occupied residential borrowers will be declined, and the non-QM loan structure typically carries higher rates and fees than conventional mortgages.
services: Construction loans for residential and small multifamily properties (12-24 month terms)
Fix-and-flip loans for short-term property renovation (6-18 month terms)
Permanent rental property financing with 30-year fully amortizing or ARM options
Short-term rental financing for Airbnb/VRBO properties based on operating history
Portfolio loans financing up to 10 rental properties under a single loan
Draw funding and reimbursement for construction costs, materials, labor, and soft costs
Loan amounts from $75,000 to $1.5 million depending on product
Flexible loan-to-cost and loan-to-value ratios (up to 92.5% LTC for fix-and-flip)
Underwriting based on rental cash flow and property value rather than employment income
Financing for single-family, townhomes, condos, and 2-10 unit investment properties
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
